source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/03_제품별 인증서 & 성적서/2. 제품별 성적서/01_HAD1717MC, MG/3. RoHS/[HAD1717MC(MG)] RoHS Test report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Intertek RoHS test report RT19R-S5146 for submitted samples HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG, dated Jan. 31, 2020, with an overall Pass result.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports RoHS restricted-substance verification for the screened components of submitted samples against Directive 2011/65/EU and Directive 2015/863 Annex II.
keyPoints: Conclusion states tested screening components of submitted samples: Pass.
Report states only components shown in the report were screened by XRF spectroscopy for Directive 2011/65/EU; other components were not screened.
Testing referenced IEC 62321 methods, XRF screening, chemical confirmation tests, and review of previous reports.
RoHS requirement table lists limits for Pb, Cd, Hg, Cr6+, PBBs, PBDEs, DBP, DEHP, BBP, and DIBP.

concepts.evidence: for RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU and Directive 2015/863 Annex II

concepts.evidence: only components shown in this report were screened by XRF spectroscopy for Directive 2011/65/EU

concepts.name: Chemical confirmation test
concepts.evidence: Chemical confirmation tests were conducted to verify the inconclusive results of XRF tests.

concepts.name: RoHS exemptions
concepts.evidence: RoHS exception: 6(c) Copper alloy containing up to 4% lead by weight

evidence.claim: The report is a test report dated Jan. 31, 2020 with report number RT19R-S5146.
The submitted sample models are HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
The tested screening components of submitted samples passed.
evidence.evidenceText: CONCLUSION: Tested Samples Result Screening components of submitted samples Pass

evidence.claim: Only components shown in the report were screened by XRF spectroscopy for Directive 2011/65/EU.
evidence.evidenceText: As requested by the applicant, only components shown in this report were screened by XRF spectroscopy for Directive 2011/65/EU. Other components were not screened in this report.

evidence.claim: The report references IEC 62321 edition 1.0, XRF screening, chemical confirmation testing, and review of previous reports for RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU and Directive 2015/863 Annex II.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to test method of IEC 62321 edition 1.0, screening by XRF spectroscopy, chemical confirmation test and review of previous reports for RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU and Directive 2015/863 Annex II

evidence.claim: RoHS limits are quoted from 2011/65/EU and 2015/863 Annex II for homogeneous material.
evidence.evidenceText: The above limits were quoted from 2011/65/EU and 2015/863 Annex II for homogeneous material.

evidence.claim: The report lists RoHS restricted substances including Pb, Cd, Hg, Cr6+, PBBs, PBDEs, DBP, DEHP, BBP, and DIBP.
evidence.evidenceText: Restricted Substances Limits Lead (Pb) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Cadmium (Cd) 0.01 % (100 mg/kg) Mercury (Hg) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Chromium (VI)(Cr6+) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Polybrominated Biphenyls (PBBs) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Polybrominated Diphenyl Ethers (PBDEs) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Dibutyl phthalate (DBP) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Di(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate (DEHP) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Benzyl butyl phthalate (BBP) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg) Diisobutyl phthalate (DIBP) 0.1 % (1000 mg/kg)

evidence.claim: Intertek states the numerical XRF data is for reference purposes only due to variation factors.
evidence.evidenceText: The numerical XRF data of test report is for reference purposes only due to the data variation incurred from various factors

evidence.claim: RoHS exemptions cited include Annex III 6(c), 6(a), 7(c)-I, and Annex IV 5.
evidence.evidenceText: RoHS exception: 6(c) Copper alloy containing up to 4% lead by weight 6(a) ... 7(c)-Ⅰ ... 5 Lead in shielding for ionising radiation

reviewFlags.reason: Revision or version metadata is not present as a distinct field in the source text.
Authority is ambiguous: the source is a laboratory test report by Intertek, not an issued regulatory authority certificate.
OCR quality for the multi-page verification tables is low; rows and columns are interleaved, so detailed per-component results should be reviewed against the original PDF.
